questions.questionText: For a mainstream HydraMaster or Sapphire Scientific truck-mount, why does the gas installation domain (AS/NZS 5601) generally NOT apply?
options: Because gas standards only apply to heavy vehicles over 4.5 t GVM
Because the manufacturer may self-certify the gas installation
Because these units recover heat from the engine exhaust through a finned-tube heat exchanger, not a fuel-gas burner
Because LPG is banned in carpet-cleaning vehicles

questions.questionText: On the manufacturer's truck-mount safety certificate, which sign-offs CANNOT be self-certified by the manufacturer and must be completed by a licensed practitioner?
options: The vehicle-modification signatory, licensed gasfitter and licensed electrician sign-offs
The WHS plant-safety design declaration
The pressure-equipment hazard-level (AS 4343) calculation
The dangerous-goods threshold assessment

questions.questionText: How is a HydraMaster Boxxer or Sapphire Scientific 370 truck-mount powered?
options: It plugs into a 240 V/50 Hz mains supply
A self-contained onboard petrol engine drives the pump and blower, with 12 V DC controls
A US 120 V supply is converted to 240 V on the machine
Mains electricity provides the heat and petrol runs only the pump

questions.questionText: What is the real Australia-versus-US difference for these truck-mount machines?
options: The machine converts 120 V to 240 V for Australian use
Australian units run on diesel instead of petrol
Australian units are mains-powered
The difference is distributor, warranty and fuel, not voltage

questions.questionText: The truck-mount's carbon monoxide monitor is set to alarm at 35 ppm. When it alarms, the operator must:
options: Evacuate to fresh air, shut the engine down and notify the supervisor
Continue for up to 30 minutes, since 35 ppm is below the danger level
Open the windows and keep cleaning
Reset the alarm and reposition the sensor

questions.questionText: Which statement about controlling carbon monoxide when running a petrol truck-mount is correct?
options: Running indoors is acceptable provided all windows and doors are open
A carport provides enough ventilation to run the unit safely
Park outdoors; opening windows and doors may not provide adequate ventilation to make an indoor space work-safe
The operator may remain in the van while the unit runs if one window is open

questions.questionText: What is the Australian workplace exposure standard for carbon monoxide?
options: 35 ppm (8-hour TWA), reducing to 20 ppm from 2026
30 ppm (8-hour TWA), reducing to 20 ppm from 2026
85 ppm (8-hour TWA)
200 ppm (8-hour TWA)

questions.questionText: Before removing any cap, valve, plug, fitting or filter on a hot, running truck-mount, the operator must:
options: Reduce pressure to half, then unscrew the fitting slowly
Keep the engine running so the pump holds the pressure steady
Wear gloves and remove the fitting at operating pressure
Shut down, cool the unit, and relieve ALL pressure before removing the fitting

questions.questionText: Under the applicable Load Restraint Guide, the secured truck-mount and tanks must stay restrained under heavy braking, resisting at minimum what forward force?
options: 0.2 g forward
0.5 g forward
0.8 g forward
2.0 g forward

questions.questionText: How must recovery (waste) water from a truck-mount be disposed of in Australia?
options: To sewer at an approved point under a trade-waste permit, never to stormwater
Down the nearest stormwater drain once the lint filter is cleaned
Onto grass or garden beds away from the street
Into any gutter, since it is treated as domestic greywater

questions.questionText: When descaling the heat exchanger, the operator should:
options: Use concentrated hydrochloric acid for the fastest result
Use only the manufacturer's descaler; never use concentrated acids or solvents such as d-limonene
Leave the descaler standing in the system overnight for a deeper clean
Substitute any available household acid at a guessed dilution

questions.questionText: Manufacturer guidance makes a water softener and regular descaling mandatory when water hardness is at or above roughly:
options: Only ever for bore or tank water, never for town supply
30 grains/gal (about 600 mg/L)
0.3 grains/gal (about 6 mg/L)
About 3.0 grains/gal (about 60 mg/L), a level exceeded across much of urban Australia

questions.questionText: Why must the trainer complete Gate A (pre-flight) against the specific unit and engine manual before the course is taught as fact?
options: Because safety-critical setpoints such as temperature, working pressure, RPM and service intervals are model- and engine-specific, and the course is a reference, not a substitute for the unit's manual
Because Gate A issues the vehicle modification plate
Because the course figures are always wrong and must be rewritten for each class
Because Safe Work Australia requires the trainer to hold an Approved Vehicle Examiner licence

questions.questionText: In the operator competency assessment, a 'not yet competent' (NYC) result on any carbon monoxide (B3) item results in:
options: A retest of only that single carbon monoxide item
A pass, provided every other section is competent
An automatic fail of the whole assessment
A conditional sign-off pending supervisor review
